title: “Content is King” — Essay by Bill Gates 1996 | by Heath Evans | Medium
excerpts:
Section Title: “Content is King” — Essay by Bill Gates 1996
Content:
Heath Evans
5 min read
·
Jan 30, 2017
--
17
Listen
Share
Ever wondered where the phrase “Content is King” stemmed from?
In January 1996, Bill Gates wrote the following essay titled “Content is King”, which was published on the Microsoft website.
*“Content is where I expect much of the real money will be made on the Internet, just as it was in broadcasting.*
*The television revolution that began half a century ago spawned a number of industries, including the manufacturing of TV sets, but the long-term winners were those who used the medium to deliver information and entertainment.*
*When it comes to an interactive network such as the Internet, the definition of “content” becomes very wide. For example, computer software is a form of content-an extremely important one, and the one that for Microsoft will remain by far the most important.*

title: Content is King, Really? - Lino Matteo's Version
excerpts:
Section Title: Content is King, Really?
Content:
Posted by [Lino Matteo](https://linomatteo.wordpress.com/author/linomatteo/) [11th May 2023 10th May 2023](https://linomatteo.wordpress.com/2023/05/11/content-is-king-really/) Posted in [Uncategorized](https://linomatteo.wordpress.com/category/uncategorised/) Tags: [Business English](https://linomatteo.wordpress.com/tag/business-english/) , [Content Is King](https://linomatteo.wordpress.com/tag/content-is-king/) , [Content Marketing](https://linomatteo.wordpress.com/tag/content-marketing/) , [Lino Matteo](https://linomatteo.wordpress.com/tag/lino-matteo/)
Content is King, Really?
“Content is King” was an essay published by Bill Gates in 1996. Keep in mind when Mr. Gates wrote his article the desktop computer ruled. WIFI and smartphones were still a decade away. Innovations and modernization of hardware have made publishing content easier, faster, and more accessible.

title: CONTENT IS KING – BILL GATES (1/3/1996)
excerpts:
CONTENT IS KING – BILL GATES (1/3/1996)
Content is where I expect much of the real money will be made on the Internet, just as it was
in broadcasting.
The television revolution that began half a century ago spawned a number of industries,
including the manufacturing of TV sets, but the long-term winners were those who used the
medium to deliver information and entertainment.
When it comes to an interactive network such as the Internet, the definition of “content”
becomes very wide. For example, computer software is a form of content-an extremely
important one, and the one that for Microsoft will remain by far the most important.
But the broad opportunities for most companies involve supplying information or
entertainment. No company is too small to participate.
One of the exciting things about the Internet is that anyone with a PC and a modem can
publish whatever content they can create. In a sense, the Internet is the multimedia equivalent

title: Bill Gates says there’s ‘no upper limit’ on AI, citing opportunity and risk – GeekWire
excerpts:
Section Title: Bill Gates says there’s ‘no upper limit’ on AI, citing opportunity and risk
Content:
“There is no upper limit on how intelligent AIs will get or on how good robots will get, and I believe the advances will not plateau before exceeding human levels,” Gates writes.
He acknowledges that missed deadlines for artificial general intelligence, or human-level AI, can “create the impression that these things will never happen.” But he warns against reaching that conclusion, arguing that bigger breakthroughs are coming, even if the timing remains uncertain.
He says he’s still optimistic overall. “As hard as last year was, I don’t believe we will slide back into the Dark Ages,” he writes. “I believe that, within the next decade, we will not only get the world back on track but enter a new era of unprecedented progress.”
But he adds that we’ll need to be “deliberate about how this technology is developed, governed, and deployed” — and that governments, not just markets, will have to

title: Bill Gates: Inside the Life and Career of the Microsoft Co-Founder - Business Insider
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > **Gates' post-Microsoft career and philanthropy**
Content:
When Gates stepped down as CEO of Microsoft, he stepped up as the co-founder of the [Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation](https://www.businessinsider.com/bill-and-melinda-gates-foundation) , the charitable organization he and his then wife Melinda Gates had founded in the year 2000. (The foundation was a revamp of the organization Gates had established in 1994 under the name the William H. Gates Foundation.)
The BMFG is a nonprofit with global reach that happens to make a tidy profit, thanks in no small part to its massive holdings of Microsoft stock. The foundation has offices around the world and is, in words from its own [site](https://www.gatesfoundation.org/) : "Guided by the belief that every life has equal value, the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation works to help all people lead healthy, productive lives."
